articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Data Portraits
articles.summary: Researchers have analyzed data from the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026 to identify key trends and patterns in match timing. The study found that teams tend to start their most critical moments, such as when a player is injured or a goal is scored, around 30 seconds before kickoff. This suggests that game timing may be more important than traditional metrics like possession or shots on target. As a result, developers building sports apps and platforms for the tournament may need to prioritize accurate match timing in their data processing.

articles.title: Helium escaping from atmosphere of nearby rocky exoplanet in a habitable zone
articles.summary: Scientists have detected helium escaping from the atmosphere of a nearby rocky exoplanet located within a habitable zone. This finding suggests that the planet's surface temperature is suitable for liquid water to exist, making it a promising candidate for hosting life. The discovery was made using data from the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite and other observations. The presence of helium could indicate the presence of volcanic activity or tectonic processes on the planet.

articles.title: Decoy Font
articles.summary: Decoy Font is a TTF font designed to hide what you're typing from AI systems, using separate spatial frequencies to communicate two different letters in the same space. The font works by overlaying thin outlines on top of each letter, creating an illusion that makes it difficult for AI models like ChatGPT to read the actual message. This simple technique can be used to protect your writing from AI scrapers and ensure that messages meant for humans are only readable by others.

articles.title: Microsoft Comic Chat is now open source
articles.summary: Microsoft Comic Chat is now open source, allowing developers to explore its core functionality that introduced informal, hand-lettered conversations within Internet Relay Chat. The chat client's speech bubbles and expressions were generated from typed text, creating a unique visual representation of online communication. This technology predates modern messaging apps with reactions, stickers, GIFs, avatars, video, and AI-generated content. Its quirky ambition and forward-thinking spirit can be seen in experiments like Comic Chat, which paved the way for more sophisticated visual representations of conversational histories.

articles.title: README, not
articles.summary: The concept of README files has been gaining traction in the programming community as a way to provide users with clear and concise information about how to use or interact with a project. A READMENOT file would serve as an unambiguous warning that the code within the project is unsuitable for direct human consumption, serving as a default explanation for why it shouldn't be read. This idea aligns with the debate surrounding what constitutes "slop" in programming, which can range from poorly developed code to lack of operator understanding. The proposed READMENOT file would provide a consistent marker for such projects, allowing users to make informed decisions about their interaction with them.

articles.title: What "Memory Compiler" Actually Means: From Bitcells to GDS Tiling
articles.summary: The memory compiler translates design parameters into views needed to tape out a chip, replicating bitcells at scale. It characterizes these cells by hand and designs them with the 6T bitcell as the most common variant. The process involves creating a T-diagram representation of the compiler's output and translating it into various views for different stages of the design flow.

articles.title: Abstracting Effects with Continuations

articles.title: SQLite should have (Rust-style) editions
articles.summary: SQLite's default foreign key constraints are often ignored by default, which can lead to dangling references and other issues in applications that rely on consistency. This is particularly problematic for projects where data integrity is crucial, such as embedded systems or financial software. The lack of enforced foreign key constraints also means that developers must manually manage user IDs and delete them when necessary, which can be error-prone and time-consuming.
